affiliations.name: Universidad Autónoma de Nayarit, Laboratorio Nacional de Investigación para la Inocuidad Alimentaria (LANIIA)-Unidad Nayarit, Calle Tres S/N, Colonia. Cd. Industrial, Z.P. 63000, Tepic, México.

All study participants signed the informed consent before samples, clinical data, and demographic information, according to a protocol approved by local bioethics commission (registry number CEBN/03/20).
The JOEM editorial board and planners have no financial interest related to this research.

Clinical significance: The present strategy can be used in different workplaces and this would serve to identify and isolate asymptomatic SARS-CoV-2-infected subjects. This methodology is highly sensitive for SARS-CoV-2 molecular detection and can be used as a suitable tool to monitor and control dispersion patterns of COVID-19 disease.
